title: leontodon saxatilis subsp. taraxacoides
intro: Leontodon saxatilis subsp. taraxacoides, also known as mountain hawk's beard, is a subspecies of the plant Leontodon saxatilis, which belongs to the dandelion family and is characterized by its yellow flowers and edible leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been used to treat wounds, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Leontodon saxatilis subsp. taraxacoides include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, making it a popular choice for herbal remedies. Leontodon saxatilis subsp. taraxacoides can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to create herbal remedies such as teas, salves, and poultices.
